title: Lomagramma matthewii
intro: Lomagramma matthewii, also known as the Matthew's lace fern, is a species of fern in the family Lomagrammaceae. The Matthew's lace fern has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in the Pacific Islands, due to its reported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which can aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and combat infections. Bioactive constituents of the plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the Matthew's lace fern is often used to make a tea or tincture, and it is also used in topical preparations such as creams and ointments to treat skin conditions and wounds.
taxonomy: Lomagramma matthewii is a species of fern in the family Lomagrammaceae. It is native to tropical regions and is classified as a vascular plant, specifically belonging to the division Pteridophyta.
